prompts.name: Greeting
prompts.expected_behavior: Kurze freundliche Antwort, zeigt dass Decode grundsätzlich funktioniert
prompts.quality_check: Antwort ist kohärent und kontextbezogen, kein Müll/Wiederholung

prompts.name: Simple Sequence
prompts.expected_behavior: Sequenz 1-10, zeigt dass Token-Generierung stabil ist
prompts.quality_check: Korrekte Zahlenfolge 1-10 ohne Auslassungen oder Wiederholungen

prompts.name: Prime Check (Python)
prompts.prompt: Write a Python function that checks if a number is prime.
prompts.quality_check: Syntaktisch korrektes Python, korrekte Primzahl-Logik (Schleife bis sqrt(n), Edge-Cases 0/1/2)

prompts.name: LRU Cache (C++)
prompts.prompt: Write a C++ class implementing a thread-safe LRU cache with get, put, and delete operations. Include proper mutex locking and comments.
prompts.expected_behavior: C++ Klasse mit std::mutex, std::unordered_map, std::list
prompts.quality_check: Kompilierbares C++, korrekte LRU-Eviction-Logik, Thread-Safety via Mutex

prompts.name: REST API (Go)
prompts.prompt: Write a REST API in Go with endpoints for user authentication (register, login, logout), JWT token handling, password hashing with bcrypt, and rate limiting middleware. Include error handling, input validation, and code comments.
prompts.expected_behavior: Strukturierter Go-Code mit HTTP-Handler, JWT-Library, bcrypt
prompts.quality_check: Go-Syntax korrekt, sinnvolle Endpoint-Struktur, Security-Basics (Hashing, JWT)

prompts.name: Mutex Explanation
prompts.prompt: Explain what a mutex is in one paragraph.
prompts.expected_behavior: Klare, präzise Erklärung von Mutual Exclusion

prompts.name: TCP vs UDP
prompts.prompt: Compare and contrast TCP and UDP protocols. Cover reliability, speed, use cases, and header differences.
prompts.expected_behavior: Strukturierter Vergleich mit korrekten technischen Details
prompts.quality_check: TCP=reliable/ordered, UDP=fast/connectionless, korrekte Use-Cases (HTTP vs DNS/Gaming)

prompts.name: GPU Architecture Blog Post
prompts.prompt: Write a detailed technical blog post about the evolution of GPU architectures from CUDA to modern compute shaders, covering parallel processing concepts, memory hierarchies, and real-world applications in machine learning and graphics.
prompts.expected_behavior: Langer kohärenter Text mit technischer Tiefe
prompts.quality_check: Historisch korrekt (CUDA 2007+), erwähnt SIMD/SIMT, Memory-Hierarchie, ML-Anwendungen

prompts.name: Binary Search Complexity
prompts.prompt: What is the time complexity of binary search and why?
prompts.expected_behavior: O(log n) mit korrekter Begründung (Halbierung pro Schritt)
prompts.quality_check: O(log n) korrekt, Begründung verständlich, optional: Best/Worst-Case

prompts.name: Debug Code
prompts.prompt: Debug this code and explain the issue: for(int i=0; i<=arr.length; i++) sum += arr[i];
prompts.quality_check: Bug korrekt identifiziert, Erklärung warum <= falsch ist, Fix vorgeschlagen

prompts.name: Distributed Message Queue
prompts.prompt: Design a distributed message queue system. Describe the architecture, how you would handle failover, message persistence, ordering guarantees, and horizontal scaling. Compare tradeoffs between at-least-once and exactly-once delivery.
prompts.quality_check: Erwähnt Partitioning, Replication, WAL/Persistence, Consumer-Groups, At-least-once vs Exactly-once Trade-offs

prompts.name: Long System Prompt + Question
prompts.prompt:
You are an expert systems architect specializing in high-performance computing, distributed systems, and GPU programming. You have extensive experience with AMD RDNA architectures, ROCm software stack, HIP programming, and CUDA-to-HIP porting. You understand memory hierarchies including L1/L2 caches, LDS (Local Data Share), and global memory access patterns. You are familiar with wave-level programming, VGPR/SGPR register management, and occupancy optimization. You have worked on inference engines for large language models and understand quantization formats like GGUF Q4_K, Q6_K, and Q8_0. Your expertise includes matrix multiplication optimization using WMMA instructions, kernel fusion strategies, and genetic algorithm-based auto-tuning. You always provide precise, technically accurate answers with concrete examples.

Given this expertise, what are the three most impactful optimizations for improving LLM decode throughput on consumer AMD GPUs?
prompts.expected_behavior: Technisch tiefe Antwort die den langen System-Prompt berücksichtigt (Prefill-Stress-Test)
prompts.quality_check: Antwort bezieht sich auf GPU/AMD-Kontext, nicht generisch. Erwähnt mindestens: Memory-Bandwidth, Quantisierung, Kernel-Fusion oder Batching

prompts.name: Long Output Story
prompts.prompt: Erzähle eine ausführliche Geschichte über einen kleinen Roboter namens Pixel, der in einer verlassenen Fabrik aufwacht und herausfinden muss, wer ihn gebaut hat.
prompts.expected_behavior: Kohärente Geschichte über ~200-400 Tokens, testet längeren Decode-Output
prompts.quality_check: Geschichte hat Anfang/Mitte/Ende, Roboter heißt Pixel, Setting=verlassene Fabrik, keine Wiederholungs-Loops

prompts.name: Arithmetic (Q4_K Precision)
prompts.quality_check: Korrekte Antwort 391. Falsche Antwort deutet auf Numerik-Problem im Q4_K-Pfad

prompts.name: Emoji/Special Characters
prompts.prompt: 🎉🎊🎈 What do these emojis mean and when would you use them?
prompts.quality_check: Emojis korrekt als Feier/Party erkannt, kohärente Antwort, kein Tokenizer-Crash
